source.description: Six-panel built-in image generation contact sheet saved under drafts/seashell-on-sand/ and cropped into raster tutorial assets.
source.notes: Backdated archive lesson kept honest in public copy; the subject teaches a fan-shaped natural object distinct from recent food, animal, and garden lessons.

process_strategy: A single six-panel contact sheet keeps one shell in the same three-quarter top view while the process adds fan shape, scalloped edge, ridges, hinge, sand marks, and restrained color before the final pass.

final_elements.name: fan shell outline
final_elements.description: A broad fan-shaped seashell silhouette with a small hinge base.

final_elements.name: scalloped outer edge
final_elements.description: Rounded bumps along the wide top edge of the shell.

final_elements.name: radiating ridges
final_elements.description: Curved ridge lines that spread from the hinge toward the scallops.

final_elements.name: hinge and lower folds
final_elements.description: A small dark hinge area with short fold marks near the shell base.

final_elements.name: sand shadow and pebbles
final_elements.description: A light cast shadow, small sand dots, and tiny pebbles around the shell.

frames.visible_job: Places the fan outline, hinge point, and light center guide.
Adds rounded scallops across the wide outer edge.
Draws radiating ridge lines from the hinge toward each scallop.
Clarifies the small hinge and lower fold marks.
Adds a soft cast shadow, small pebbles, sand dots, and light peach shading.

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens the shell outline, ridges, hinge, sand marks, shadow, and restrained peach shading already established.
allowed_changes: small line cleanup on established elements

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
